front: Depois de tanta discussão, eles resolveram colocar os pingos nos is.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-tah dees-koo-SOHNG, EH-leez rreh-sohl-VEH-roong koh-loh-KAHR ooz PEENG-goohz noohz EEZ.
back: After so much discussion, they decided to dot the i's and cross the t's.
context: Using 'colocar os pingos nos is' to mean clarifying every detail or setting the record straight.

front: Não adianta tentar dourar a pílula; a situação financeira é realmente grave.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR doh-RAHR ah PEE-loo-lyah; ah see-too-ah-ZOHNG fee-nahn-SAY-rah eh rre-ahl-MEHN-tee GRAH-vee.
back: It's no use trying to sugarcoat the pill; the financial situation is truly serious.
context: Using 'dourar a pílula' (to gild the pill) to mean trying to make bad news sound better than it is.

front: Ele é um pão-duro que se recusa a pagar até uma xícara de café para os amigos.
pronunciation: EH-lee eh oong PONG-DOO-roo keh see rreh-KOO-zah ah pah-GAHR ah-TEH OO-mah SHEE-kah-rah jee kah-FEH PAH-rah ooz ah-MEE-goohz.
back: He is a cheapskate who refuses to pay even for a cup of coffee for his friends.
context: Using 'pão-duro' (hard bread) to describe a stingy, tightfisted person.

front: A conversa estava ótima, mas tive que cortar o barato deles avisando sobre a reunião.
pronunciation: ah kohn-VEHR-sah ees-TAH-vah OH-chee-mah, mahz CHEE-vee keh kohr-TAHR oh bah-RAH-too DEH-leez ah-vee-ZAHN-doh SOH-bree ah rrehoo-nee-ONG.
back: The conversation was great, but I had to spoil their fun by warning them about the meeting.
context: Using 'cortar o barato' to mean killing someone's buzz, spoiling the fun, or raining on their parade.

front: Eu ia comprar aquela TV, mas desisti quando vi o vendedor tentando me passar a perna.
pronunciation: ew EE-ah kohm-PRAHR ah-KEH-lyah teh-VEH, mahz jee-zees-CHEE KWAHN-doh VEE oh vehn-deh-DOHR tehn-TAHN-doh mee pah-SAHR ah PEHR-nah.
back: I was going to buy that TV, but I backed out when I saw the salesman trying to pull a fast one on me.
context: Using 'passar a perna' (to cross the leg over someone) to mean tricking, cheating, or deceiving someone.

front: Não se preocupe com a apresentação de amanhã; você já está com o burro na sombra.
pronunciation: NOHNG see pree-oh-KOO-pee kohm ah ah-preh-zehn-tah-ZOHNG jee ah-mang-YAH; voh-SEH ZHAH ees-TAH kohm oh BOO-rroo nah SOHM-brah.
back: Don't worry about tomorrow's presentation; you've already got it made.
context: Using 'estar com o burro na sombra' (to have one's donkey in the shade) to mean being in a secure, comfortable, or successful position.

front: O projeto novo é ótimo, mas tirar essa ideia do papel vai dar pano pra manga.
pronunciation: oh proh-ZHEH-toh NOH-voo eh OH-chee-moo, mahz tee-RAHR EHS-sah ee-DAY-ah doh pah-PEHL VAY dahr PONG prah MAN-gah.
back: The new project is great, but bringing this idea to life is going to give us a lot to talk about and deal with.
context: Using 'dar pano pra manga' (to give cloth for sleeves) to describe a topic or task that will generate a lot of discussion, debate, or work.

front: Ele sempre some nos momentos difíceis e deixa o abacaxi para nós descascarmos.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree SOH-mee noohz moh-MEHN-toohz jee-FEE-seeez ee DAY-shah oh ah-bah-kah-SHEE PAH-rah NOHZ dees-kahs-KAHR-moohz.
back: He always disappears during tough times and leaves the mess for us to clean up.
context: Using 'descascar um abacaxi' (to peel a pineapple) to mean solving a very difficult or annoying problem.

front: Achei que o teste seria moleza, mas acabei quebrando a cara por não ter estudado.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh oh TEHS-chee seh-REE-ah moh-LEH-zah, mahz ah-kah-BAY kweh-BRAHN-doh ah KAH-rah pohr NOHNG tehr ees-too-DAH-doh.
back: I thought the test would be a breeze, but I ended up falling flat on my face because I didn't study.
context: Using 'quebrar a cara' (to break one's face) to mean failing, getting hurt, or being proven wrong after an overconfident assumption.

front: Naquela reunião importante, ele acabou enfiando os pés pelas mãos ao tentar se explicar.
pronunciation: nah-KEH-lyah rrehoo-nee-ONG eem-pohr-TAHN-tee, EH-lee ah-kah-BOH eheng-fee-AHN-doh ooz PAYS PEH-lahz MAHEEZ_oo tehn-TAHR see ees-plee-KAHR.
back: In that important meeting, he ended up bumbling and getting completely tangled up while trying to explain himself.
context: Using 'enfiar os pés pelas mãos' (to put feet through hands) to mean acting in a clumsy, disorganized, or confused manner.

front: O diretor gosta de manter todos sob controle e quer saber de tudo tim-tim por tim-tim.
pronunciation: oh jee-reh-TOH_r GOHS-tah jee mahn-TEHR TOH-doohz SOHB kohn-TROH-lee ee KEHR sah-BEHR jee TOO-doh cheeng-CHEENG pohr cheeng-CHEENG.
back: The director likes to keep everyone under control and wants to know everything down to the smallest detail.
context: Using 'tim-tim por tim-tim' to mean explaining or wanting to know something in precise detail.

front: Ela tentou me convencer com aquela história, mas eu sei muito bem onde aperta o sapato.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee kohn-vehn-SEHR kohm ah-KEH-lyah ees-TOH-ryah, mahz ew SAY MWEE-too BEHNG OHN-jee ah-PEHR-tah oh sah-PAH-too.
back: She tried to convince me with that story, but I know very well where the real trouble lies.
context: Using 'saber onde aperta o sapato' (to know where the shoe pinches) to mean knowing where the actual difficulty or vulnerability is.

front: Não compre esse celular usado sem testar antes, senão você pode comprar gato por lebre.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree EHS-see seh-loo-LAHR oo-ZAH-doh sehng chehs-TAHR AHN-teez, see-NONG voh-SEH POH-jee kohm-PRAHR GAH-too pohr LEH-bree.
back: Don't buy this used phone without testing it first, otherwise you might get ripped off.
context: Using 'comprar gato por lebre' (to buy a cat instead of a hare) to mean being cheated by buying a counterfeit or inferior product.

front: Depois de tantas mentiras, as desculpas dele não colam mais com ninguém.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-tahz meheng-CHEE-rahz, az dees-KOOL-pahz DEH-lee NOHNG KOH-lahoo_mahy_Z kohm neheng-GEHNG.
back: After so many lies, his excuses don't wash with anyone anymore.
context: Using the verb 'colar' (literally 'to glue') colloquially to mean an excuse or story being believable or accepted.

front: A discussão sobre futebol na mesa do bar acabou terminando em pizza.
pronunciation: ah dees-koo-SOHNG SOH-bree foo-chee-BOHL nah MEH-sah doh BAHR ah-kah-BOH tehr-mee-NAHN-doh ehng PEE-tsah.
back: The soccer argument at the bar table ended up ending in nothing with no consequences.
context: Using 'terminar em pizza' (to end in pizza) to describe a situation, especially a political or social scandal, that ends without any resolution or punishment.

front: Ele se acha a última bolacha do pacote só porque fala três idiomas.
pronunciation: EH-lee see AH-shah ah OOL-chee-mah boh-LAH-shah doh pah-KOH-chee SOH pohr-KEH FAH-lah TRAY_Z ee-jee-OH-mahz.
back: He thinks he is God's gift to the world just because he speaks three languages.
context: Using 'a última bolacha do pacote' (the last cookie in the pack) to describe someone who thinks they are highly superior or irreplaceable.

front: Ela tentou dar um nó cego na fiscalização, mas acabou sendo multada mesmo assim.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr oong NOH SEH-goo nah fees-kah-lee-zah-SOHNG, mahz ah-kah-BOH SEHN-doh mool-TAH-dah MEHS-moo ah-SEENG.
back: She tried to outsmart the inspection, but ended up being fined anyway.
context: Using 'dar um nó cego' (to tie a blind knot) to mean creating an extremely complex, unsolvable problem, or cleverly dodging rules.

front: Ele adora contar vantagem sobre seus investimentos, mas todo mundo sabe que é exagero.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah kohn-TAHR vahn-TAH-zheng SOH-bree SEW_Z eeng-vehs-chee-MEHN-toohz, mahz TOH-doh MOON-doo SAH-bee keh eh ee-zah-ZHEH-roo.
back: He loves to brag about his investments, but everyone knows it's an exaggeration.
context: Using 'contar vantagem' to mean bragging, boasting, or showing off.

front: O trânsito hoje estava tão ruim que quase perdi as estribeiras no caminho.
pronunciation: oh TRAN-zee-too HOY-zhee ees-TAH-vah TOHNG rroo-EEG keh KWAH-zee pehr-JEE ahz ees-tree-BAY-rahz noo kah-MEENG-yoo.
back: The traffic today was so bad that I almost lost my temper on the way.
context: Using 'perder as estribeiras' (to lose the stirrups) to mean losing self-control, patience, or temper.

front: Ela não faz nenhum esforço na faculdade, só quer saber de levar a vida na flauta.
pronunciation: EH-lyah NOHNG FAHZ neheng-GOONG ees-FOHR-soo nah fah-kool-DAH-jee, SOH KEHR sah-BEHR jee leh-VAHR ah VEE-dah nah FLOW-tah.
back: She doesn't make any effort in college, she just wants to coast through life effortlessly.
context: Using 'levar na flauta' (to play it on the flute) to mean taking things carelessly, lazily, or without serious effort.

front: Quando meu computador quebrou no meio do trabalho, tive que me virar nos trinta.
pronunciation: KWAHN-doh MEW kohm-poo-tah-DOH_r kweh-BROH noo MAY-oo doh trah-BAH-lyoo, CHEE-vee keh mee vee-RAHR noohz TREEN-tah.
back: When my computer broke in the middle of work, I had to manage and solve it quickly under pressure.
context: Using 'se virar nos trinta' (to manage within thirty seconds) to mean performing a quick improvisation or solving a tough issue with very little time or resources.

front: A nova funcionária é uma mão na roda; ela organiza os arquivos em poucos minutos.
pronunciation: ah NOH-vah foon-syo-NAH-ryah eh OO-mah MONG nah RROH-dah; EH-lyah ohr-gah-NEE-zah ooz ahr-KEE-voohz ehng POH-koohz mee-NOO-toohz.
back: The new employee is a lifesaver; she organizes the files in just a few minutes.
context: Using 'uma mão na roda' (a hand on the wheel) to describe something or someone extremely helpful, practical, or convenient.

front: Ele sempre tenta tapar o buraco do orçamento usando o limite do cheque especial.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree TEHN-tah tah-PAHR oh boo-RAH-koo doh ohr-sah-MEHN-too oo-ZAHN-doh oh lee-MEE-chee doh SHEH-kee ees-peh-SYAHL.
back: He always tries to patch up the budget deficit by using the overdraft limit.
context: Using 'tapar o buraco' (to plug the hole) to mean temporarily resolving a financial gap or shortage.

front: Depois de tanto tempo trabalhando sem descanso, ele acabou entregando os pontos.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-too TEHM-poo trah-bah-lyAHN-doh sehng dees-KAN-soo, EH-lee ah-kah-BOH een-treh-GAHN-doh ooz POHN-toohz.
back: After so much time working without rest, he ended up throwing in the towel.
context: Using 'entregar os pontos' (to hand over the points) to mean giving up, surrendering, or admitting defeat.

front: Ela comprou um carro novo sem ter como pagar, pondo a carroça na frente dos bois.
pronunciation: EH-lyah kohm-PROH oong KAHR-rroo NOH-voo sehng TEHR KOH-mou pah-GAHR, POHN-doh ah kahr-ROH-sah nah FREHN-chee doohz BOY_Z.
back: She bought a new car without having the means to pay, putting the cart before the horse.
context: Using 'pôr a carroça na frente dos bois' to describe doing things in the wrong chronological or logical order.

front: Ele acha que vai conseguir aquela vaga sem experiência, mas está chovendo no molhado.
pronunciation: EH-lee AH-shah keh VAY kohn-seh-GHEE ah-KEH-lyah VAH-gah sehng ees-peh-RYEHN-syah, mahz ees-TAH sho-VEHN-doh noo moh-LYAH-doh.
back: He thinks he will get that opening without experience, but he is beating a dead horse.
context: Using 'chover no molhado' (to rain on what is already wet) to mean wasting time repeating something obvious or doing futile work.

front: Aquele candidato gosta de gesticular muito e falar bonito para encher linguiça.
pronunciation: ah-KEH-lee kahn-jee-DAH-too GOHS-tah jee zhes-chee-koo-LAHR MWEE-too ee fah-LAHR boo-NEE-too PAH-rah een-SHEHR leeng-GWEE-sah.
back: That candidate likes to gesture a lot and speak beautifully just to pad his speech with fluff.
context: Using 'encher linguiça' (to stuff sausage) to mean talking or writing at length without saying anything of actual substance; padding.

front: Não perca tempo discutindo com ele; é apenas perda de tempo e gastar saliva.
pronunciation: NOHNG PEHR-kah TEHM-poo dees-koo-CHEENG-doh kohm EH-lee; eh ah-PEH-nahz PEHR-dah jee TEHM-poo ee gahs-TAHR sah-LEE-vah.
back: Don't waste time arguing with him; it is just a waste of time and breath.
context: Using 'gastar saliva' (to waste saliva) to mean talking or arguing in vain.

front: Nossa equipe conseguiu dar a volta por cima após perder o principal cliente.
pronunciation: NOHS-sah ee-KEE-pee kohn-seh-GHEW dahr ah VOHL-tah pohr SEE-mah AH-pohz pehr-DEHR oh preeng-see-PAHL klee-EHN-chee.
back: Our team managed to make a comeback after losing our main client.
context: Using 'dar a volta por cima' (to turn around over the top) to mean recovering from a setback or overcoming adversity successfully.

front: Ela tentou me fazer de bobo, mas eu não nasci ontem e percebi o plano.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee fah-ZEHR jee BOH-boo, mahz ew NOHNG nah-SHEE OHN-cheh ee pehr-seh-BEE oh PLAH-noo.
back: She tried to play me for a fool, but I wasn't born yesterday and I noticed the plan.
context: Using 'não nascer ontem' to indicate that one is not naive or easily fooled.

front: Esse assunto já foi resolvido há semanas; não vale a pena desenterrar defunto.
pronunciation: EHS-see ah-SOON-too ZHAH FOY rreh-sohl-VEE-doh AH seh-MAH-nahz; NOHNG VAH-lee ah PEH-nah deez-ehn-tehr-RAHR jee-FOON-too.
back: This matter was already resolved weeks ago; it's not worth bringing up old issues.
context: Using 'desenterrar defunto' (to dig up a corpse) to mean bringing up dead, past, or long-settled issues/arguments.

front: Fui pego de calças curtas quando o professor me fez aquela pergunta complexa.
pronunciation: fwee PEH-goo jee KAHL-sahz KOOR-tahz KWAHN-doh oh proh-fehs-SOH_r mee FEZ ah-KEH-lyah pehr-GOON-tah kohm-PLEH-ksah.
back: I was caught completely off guard when the professor asked me that complex question.
context: Using 'pegar de calças curtas' (to catch in short trousers) to mean catching someone unprepared or off guard.

front: Se você continuar agindo assim, vai acabar queimando o seu filme com a gerência.
pronunciation: see voh-SEH kohn-chee-NWAHR ah-ZHEENG-doh ah-SEENG, VAY ah-kah-BAHR kay-MAHN-doh oh SEW FEEL-mee kohm ah zheh-REHN-syah.
back: If you keep acting like this, you will end up ruining your reputation with management.
context: Using 'queimar o filme' (to burn the film) to mean damaging one's reputation or image in front of others.

front: Ele adora meter o bedelho onde não é chamado e dar palpites sobre a vida alheia.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah meh-TEHR oh beh-DEH-lyoo OHN-jee NOHNG eh shah-MAH-dooh ee dahr pahl-PEE-cheez SOH-bree ah VEE-dah ahl-YAY-ah.
back: He loves to stick his nose where he is not wanted and give opinions on other people's lives.
context: Using 'meter o bedelho' (to stick the latch/bolt) to mean meddling or noseying into someone else's business.

front: Minha avó é uma fofa, mas às vezes fala coisas que me deixam sem graça.
pronunciation: MEENG-yah ah-VOH eh OO-mah FOH-fah, mahz ahz VEH-zeez FAH-lah KOY-zahz keh mee DAY-shong sehng GRAH-sah.
back: My grandmother is a sweetheart, but sometimes she says things that make me feel awkward.
context: Using 'ficar sem graça' to mean feeling awkward, embarrassed, or self-conscious.

front: O preço daquele apartamento no centro é de cair o queixo.
pronunciation: oh PREH-soo dah-KEH-lee ah-pahr-tah-MEHN-too noo SEHN-troo eh jee kah-EER oh KAY-shoo.
back: The price of that apartment downtown is jaw-dropping.
context: Using 'de cair o queixo' (jaw-dropping / chin-falling) to describe something highly shocking, impressive, or surprising.

front: Pare de chover no molhado e me diga de uma vez qual é a sua proposta.
pronunciation: PAH-ree jee sho-VEHR noo moh-LYAH-doh ee mee JEE-gah jee OO-mah VEZ kwahl eh ah SOO-ah proh-POHS-tah.
back: Stop repeating yourself and tell me once and for all what your proposal is.
context: Another common application of 'chover no molhado', emphasizing useless repetition of words.

front: Ela comprou aquela bolsa cara só para fazer inveja para as colegas de trabalho.
pronunciation: EH-lyah kohm-PROH ah-KEH-lyah BOHL-sah KAH-rah SOH PAH-rah fah-ZEHR eeng-VEH-zha_PAH-rah az koh-LEH-gahz jee trah-BAH-lyoo.
back: She bought that expensive purse just to show off and make her coworkers jealous.
context: Using 'fazer inveja' to mean intentionally showing off to make others feel envious.

front: Ele achou que conseguiria passar na prova sem estudar, mas quebrou a cara feio.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-SHOH keh kohn-seh-ghee-REE-ah pah-SAHR nah PROH-vah sehng ees-too-DAHR, mahz kweh-BROH ah KAH-rah FAY-oo.
back: He thought he would manage to pass the test without studying, but he failed spectacularly.
context: Using 'quebrar a cara feio' to emphasize an embarrassing or hard failure.

front: A ideia do piquenique no parque foi por água abaixo por causa da chuva repentina.
pronunciation: ah ee-DAY-ah doh pee-kee-NEE-kee noo PAHR-kee FOY pohr AH-gwah ah-BAY-shoo pohr KOW-zah dah SHOO-vah rreh-pehn-TEE-nah.
back: The idea of a picnic in the park fell through because of the sudden rain.
context: Using 'ir por água abaixo' (to go down the water/drain) to mean a plan or project failing or falling through.

front: Não ligue para o que ele diz, ele adora falar bobagem para chamar a atenção.
pronunciation: NOHNG LEE-ghee PAH-rah oh keh EH-lee JEEZ, EH-lee ah-DOH-rah fah-LAHR boh-BAH-zheng PAH-rah shah-MAHR ah ah-tehn-SOHNG.
back: Don't pay attention to what he says, he loves to talk nonsense to get attention.
context: Using 'falar bobagem' to mean talking nonsense, rubbish, or saying silly things.

front: Depois de receber a promoção, ele passou a olhar todo mundo de cima para baixo.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee rreh-seh-BEHR ah proh-moh-ZOHNG, EH-lee pah-SOH_ah oh-LYAHR TOH-doh MOON-doo jee SEE-mah PAH-rah BAY-shoo.
back: After receiving the promotion, he started looking down on everyone.
context: Using 'olhar de cima para baixo' (to look from top to bottom) to mean looking down on someone condescendingly.

front: Eu queria contar a surpresa, mas meu irmão acabou dando com a língua nos dentes.
pronunciation: ew kee-REE-ah kohn-TAHR ah soor-PREH-zah, mahz MEW eer-MONG ah-kah-BOH DAHN-doh kohm ah LEENG-gwah noohz DEHN-teez.
back: I wanted to tell the surprise, but my brother ended up letting the cat out of the bag.
context: Another context for 'dar com a língua nos dentes' (spilling secrets).

front: Não se preocupe, essa tarefa é mamão com açúcar e você terminará em dez minutos.
pronunciation: NOHNG see pree-oh-KOO-pee, EHS-sah tah-REH-fah eh mah-MONG kohm ah-SOO-kahr ee voh-SEH tehr-mee-nah-RAH ehng DEZ mee-NOO-toohz.
back: Don't worry, this task is a piece of cake and you will finish it in ten minutes.
context: Using 'mamão com açúcar' (papaya with sugar) to describe something incredibly easy.

front: Ele prometeu mundos e fundos durante a campanha, mas não cumpriu quase nada.
pronunciation: EH-lee proh-meh-TEW MOON-doohz ee FOON-doohz doo-RAHN-tee ah kahm-PAH-nyah, mahz NOHNG koom-PREW KWAH-zee NAH-dah.
back: He promised the moon and the stars during the campaign, but fulfilled almost nothing.
context: Using 'prometer mundos e fundos' (to promise worlds and depths) to mean making extravagant, unrealistic promises.
